sourceMovie.title: Planes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Planes (2013) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Planes clicked for you, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Planes clicked for you, Chicken Run (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Planes?
faq.answer: Try Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit, Chicken Run, The Little Prince—each shares DNA with Planes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Planes?
faq.answer: Planes (2013) is best described as Animation, Family, Adventure, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Planes part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Planes Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
